papers_mined.title: Haka (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Maori posture-dance: group performance with vigorous movements and stamping of feet (waewae takahia) + rhythmically shouted accompaniment + pukana bulging eyes + whetero tongue.

papers_mined.title: Kapa haka (Wikipedia)
papers_mined.note: Synchrony essential: each performer linked in step to avoid throwing off group; warriors performed tutungarahu to indicate solidarity before battle; out-of-sync read as bad omen.

papers_mined.title: Haka definition history meaning (Britannica)
papers_mined.note: Synchronized movements include loud chants, foot-stamping, thigh-slapping, striking facial expressions; collective coordination + unified expression.

papers_mined.title: Haka guide New Zealand traditional Maori dance
papers_mined.note: Group-led haka requires precise coordinated synchronization to demonstrate unity and harmony of the collective.

papers_mined.title: PEPE: Long-context Extension for LLMs (Findings EMNLP 2025)
papers_mined.note: Periodic phase extension for RoPE in long-context LLMs; explicit periodic phase reset/adjustment of rotation.

papers_mined.title: Phase-Aligned RoPE Mixed-Resolution Diffusion Transformer 2511.19778
papers_mined.note: CRPA Cross-Resolution Phase-Aligned Attention modifies RoPE index map so all Q/K positions are expressed on the query's stride; equal physical distances induce identical phases.

papers_mined.title: Preplan-and-Anchor Rhythm 2510.13554
papers_mined.note: Attention rhythm with periodic anchor tokens (punctuation marks) consistently attracting elevated attention weights across layers; serves as natural syntactic boundaries forming recurrent connections.

papers_mined.title: SyncLLM University of Washington
papers_mined.note: Trains models to generate interleaved chunks with periodic synchronization tokens.
